content.text:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the entire codebase and the UX redesign document. Let me formulate the detailed implementation plan. --- # Tier 2 Implementation Plan: Sessions & Insights Redesign ## Overview This plan covers 6 scope items for Tier 2, ordered by dependency and implementability. I will address each design decision explicitly, then provide a task breakdown. --- ## 1. URL-Persisted Filters (Foundation -- Must Come First) This item is a **prerequisite** for several others. The sidebar layout links back to filtered views (e.g., "Same project (5 sessions)"), and the Insights view modes should persist in URL. Doing this first means all subsequent items can build on URL state. ### Design Decision: Custom hook vs inline useSearchParams **Recommendation: Custom `useFilterParams` hook.** Rationale: - Both SessionsPage (5 filters) and InsightsPage (4 filters + view mode) need the same pattern. - Inline `useSearchParams` calls in each page would duplicate serialization/deserialization logic. - A single hook encapsulates: reading from URL, writing to URL, providing defaults without URL pollution. ### Approach Create a generic `useFilterParams` hook in `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/dashboard/src/hooks/useFilterParams.ts`: ```typescript // Hook signature function useFilterParams>( defaults: T ): [T, (updates: Partial) => void, () => void] ``` The hook: - Reads current `searchParams` via `useSearchParams()` from react-router v7 - Returns merged values (URL overrides defaults) - Setter function calls `setSearchParams`, only including non-default values (keeping the URL clean) - Clear function resets to defaults (removes all query params) ### Which filters persist **SessionsPage** -- ALL filters: - `q` (search text) - `project` (project ID) - `source` (source tool) - `character` (session character) - `status` (analyzed/unanalyzed) **InsightsPage** -- ALL filters + view mode: - `q` (search text) - `project` (project ID) - `type` (insight type) - `view` (timeline/type/project/session -- the new view modes) ### Default handling Defaults are NOT written to the URL. The hook only includes params that differ from defaults. So `/sessions` and `/sessions?project=all` are equivalent, but only the former shows in the URL bar. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/hooks/useFilterParams.ts` | **NEW** | ~50 | | `dashboard/src/pages/SessionsPage.tsx` | Modify -- replace 5 `useState` with `useFilterParams` | ~30 lines changed | | `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Modify -- replace 4 `useState` with `useFilterParams` | ~25 lines changed | ### Complexity Low-medium. The hook is straightforward. The page modifications are mechanical replacements of `useState` with the hook's return values. The trickiest part is ensuring `setSearchParams` uses `replace: true` for filter changes (not `push`, to avoid polluting browser history with every keystroke in the search input -- use a debounce or `replace` mode). **Implementation note:** For the search input, use `replace: true` in `setSearchParams` to avoid cluttering the history stack. For dropdown filter changes, `push` is fine since those are discrete user actions. --- ## 2. Prompt Quality Progress Ring This is the **simplest** and most self-contained item. Zero dependencies on other items. ### Design Decision: SVG custom component vs Recharts RadialBarChart **Recommendation: Custom SVG component (`ProgressRing`).** Rationale: - Recharts RadialBarChart is overkill for a single-value circular gauge. It comes with axis system, tooltip, legend infrastructure that would need to be suppressed. - A custom SVG component is ~40 lines, zero-dependency (no recharts import), and gives full control over styling. - The existing PromptQualityCard already has a `getScoreColor` function that maps score to tailwind color classes. The SVG ring needs hex values instead, but we can maintain a parallel mapping. ### Approach Create `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/dashboard/src/components/shared/ProgressRing.tsx`: ```typescript interface ProgressRingProps { value: number; // 0-100 size?: number; // default 64 strokeWidth?: number; // default 6 className?: string; } ``` The component renders: - An SVG with two circles: a background track (muted color) and a foreground arc (colored by score) - The score number centered inside - Uses `strokeDasharray` and `strokeDashoffset` for the arc - Color mapped from score: >=80 green, >=60 yellow, >=40 orange, <40 red (matching existing `getScoreColor`) - Dark mode: uses CSS variables or hard-coded hex values that work in both themes ### Placement in PromptQualityCard Replace the current text-based score display (lines 92-97 in PromptQualityCard.tsx): ```tsx // Current:

{score}

/100

// New: ``` The rest of the card layout stays the same. The ring replaces the number+"/100" display while keeping the label/description to its right. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/components/shared/ProgressRing.tsx` | **NEW** | ~45 | | `dashboard/src/components/insights/PromptQualityCard.tsx` | Modify -- swap score display for ProgressRing | ~10 lines changed | ### Complexity Low. Self-contained component, minimal integration surface. --- ## 3. Session Detail Sidebar Layout ### Design Decision: shadcn ResizablePanel vs fixed-width CSS sidebar **Recommendation: Fixed-width CSS sidebar (no ResizablePanel).** Rationale: - ResizablePanel (from `react-resizable-panels`) is not installed. It would be a new dependency (`radix-ui` v1.4.3 is installed but ResizablePanel comes from a separate `react-resizable-panels` package). - The sidebar content has a known, fixed structure (vitals, insight summary, navigation). There is no user need to resize it. - A simple flexbox layout (`flex flex-row` with a `w-72 shrink-0` sidebar and `flex-1` main) is sufficient and zero-dependency. - The UX doc specifies 280px (w-70) which is close to `w-72` (288px) in Tailwind. ### Layout structure ```
{/* Sidebar -- hidden on mobile, shown on lg+ */}
{/* Main content */}
{/* Header stays here */} {/* Tabs stay here */}
``` ### Mobile collapse On screens below `lg` (1024px), the sidebar is hidden via `hidden lg:flex`. The vitals data is already shown in the header metadata row and the Overview tab's Session Vitals grid, so mobile users don't lose access to the data. No separate mobile pill strip is needed for Tier 2 -- that is a Tier 3 polish item. ### SessionSidebar component content Extract from the current Overview tab into the sidebar: - **Session Vitals**: Duration, Messages (with user/assistant breakdown), Tool calls, Character - **Cost & Model**: Estimated cost, Primary model, All models used - **Branch & Source**: Git branch, Source tool - **Outcome**: Outcome badge (if analyzed) - **Insight Summary**: Count by type (e.g., "2 Learnings, 1 Decision"), clickable to scroll main content - **Quick Navigate**: "Same project" link (links to `/sessions?project=` -- uses URL-persisted filters from item 1) ### What stays in the main content Overview tab - Summary section (with bullets) - PR links section - Insights (learnings, decisions, prompt quality) -- the actual cards The vitals grid (currently 4 cards) and Usage Stats section are **removed from Overview** and moved to sidebar. This reduces the Overview scroll depth significantly. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/components/sessions/SessionSidebar.tsx` | **NEW** | ~150 | | `dashboard/src/pages/SessionDetailPage.tsx` | Modify -- wrap in flex layout, move vitals to sidebar, remove vitals from overview | ~80 lines changed (significant restructure) | ### Complexity Medium. The SessionDetailPage is 640 lines and the restructure touches the outer layout (changing from single-column to two-column) and moves significant JSX blocks. But it is a pure layout change with no logic changes. ### Dependencies Depends on Item 1 (URL-persisted filters) for the "Same project" navigation link in the sidebar. --- ## 4. Insights Page View Modes ### Design Decision: How to implement the mode switcher **Recommendation: Tabs-like segmented control using shadcn Tabs component (already installed).** The existing `Tabs` component from shadcn/ui is already used in SessionDetailPage. Use it for the view mode selector: ```tsx Timeline By Type By Project By Session ``` The `viewMode` comes from `useFilterParams` (URL-persisted, default: "timeline"). ### Grouping logic per mode All grouping is **client-side** since `useInsights()` already fetches all insights. No API changes needed. **Timeline (default):** Group by date (Today, Yesterday, This Week, Earlier -- matching the pattern in SessionsPage's `getDateGroup`). Uses the existing chronological order. **By Type:** Group insights by `type` field. Within each group, sort by `timestamp` DESC. Show sections with headers: "Decisions (8)", "Learnings (12)", etc. Each section is a collapsible container. Type-specific cards render within their section. **By Project:** Group insights by `project_name`. Within each group, sort by `timestamp` DESC. Show project name as section header with insight count. **By Session:** Group insights by `session_id`. Need to show session title for each group. The insight objects already have `session_id` but not session title. Two options: - Option A: Use `session_id` as the group key and show the first insight's `project_name` + timestamp as the section header (approximate, since we don't have session title in the insight response) - Option B: Cross-reference with sessions data by also calling `useSessions()` to get titles **Recommendation: Option A** for simplicity. We can show "Session from [project_name] -- [formatted timestamp]" as the group header. Fetching all sessions just for titles adds an unnecessary query. ### View mode URL persistence The `view` param is included in `useFilterParams` for InsightsPage. Default is `timeline` (not written to URL). Switching to "type" sets URL to `/insights?view=type`. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Modify -- add view mode tabs, grouping logic, grouped rendering | ~150 lines added | | `dashboard/src/lib/utils.ts` | Modify -- extract `getDateGroup` from SessionsPage to shared utility (or duplicate) | ~15 lines | ### Complexity Medium. The grouping logic is straightforward `Array.reduce` / `Map` operations. The rendering is repetitive (each mode renders the same InsightCard/InsightListItem components, just grouped differently). The bulk of the work is in the InsightsPage template changes. ### Dependencies Depends on Item 1 (URL-persisted filters) for persisting the view mode. --- ## 5. Conversation Search ### Design Decision: Client-side vs server-side search **Recommendation: Client-side search with "load all messages" approach.** Rationale: - The current `useMessages` hook uses infinite scroll (50 messages per page). For search, we need all messages loaded. - Adding a server-side search endpoint would require: new Hono route, SQLite FTS setup (or LIKE query), and a different response format. This is significant effort for a Tier 2 item. - Client-side approach: when the user activates search, load all remaining messages (call `fetchNextPage` until done), then filter client-side. - Most sessions have <200 messages. Even at 200 messages, the data payload is manageable. ### Approach Add a search bar at the top of the Conversation tab in SessionDetailPage: ```tsx // In the Conversation TabsContent
setSearchMatches(matchIndices)} />
``` **ConversationSearch component:** - Text input with search icon - When user types, searches across `message.content` using case-insensitive `includes` - Shows match count ("3 of 12 matches") - Up/Down arrow buttons to navigate between matches - Current match index tracked in state - When navigating to a match, scroll to the corresponding `#msg-{id}` element (already exists in ChatConversation.tsx line 88) **Load-all behavior:** - When search is activated (first character typed), if `hasMore` is true, trigger a loop to load all pages. Show a subtle loading indicator while loading. - Once all messages are loaded, search proceeds client-side. - A ref tracks whether full load has been triggered to avoid re-loading. **Highlight approach:** - The ConversationSearch component passes a `highlightTerm` prop down to ChatConversation - ChatConversation passes it to MessageBubble - MessageBubble's markdown rendering applies a `` wrapper around matching text segments - This is non-trivial for the markdown renderer. **Simpler alternative:** just scroll to matching messages and add a visual indicator (e.g., a left border highlight) without inline text highlighting. This is sufficient for Tier 2. **Recommendation for Tier 2:** Scroll-to-match with message-level highlight (border/background), NOT inline text highlighting. Inline highlighting in markdown-rendered content is a Tier 3 item. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/components/chat/conversation/ConversationSearch.tsx` | **NEW** | ~80 | | `dashboard/src/components/chat/conversation/ChatConversation.tsx` | Modify -- accept `highlightMessageId` prop, apply highlight class | ~15 lines changed | | `dashboard/src/pages/SessionDetailPage.tsx` | Modify -- add search state, pass to ChatConversation | ~25 lines changed | ### Complexity Medium. The search logic itself is simple. The tricky parts are: 1. Loading all messages when search activates (managing the "load all" loop) 2. Scrolling to specific messages (using `document.getElementById('msg-...')?.scrollIntoView()`) 3. Keeping the search bar sticky while the conversation scrolls ### Dependencies None -- this is self-contained within SessionDetailPage. --- ## 6. Recurring Patterns Section ### Design Decision: How to group linked insights **Recommendation: Client-side grouping from `linked_insight_ids` field.** The `linked_insight_ids` field is a JSON-encoded string array on each insight. Insights that share linked IDs are part of the same recurring pattern. The grouping logic: 1. Parse `linked_insight_ids` for each insight 2. Build a Union-Find / adjacency structure: if insight A lists insight B, they are in the same group 3. From each group, extract: the theme (from the insight title), the count, the project names, the most recent timestamp **Alternative:** The `findRecurringInsights` API already exists (POST `/api/analysis/recurring`). It uses LLM to identify recurring patterns. However: - This requires LLM to be configured - It is an active analysis operation, not a read operation - It returns groups and updates `linked_insight_ids` in the database **Recommendation:** Use the already-stored `linked_insight_ids` for the display (client-side grouping). Show a "Find Patterns" button that triggers the `findRecurringInsights` API to populate/refresh the linked IDs. This way the section works with existing data and can be refreshed on demand. ### Where to show Show at the top of InsightsPage, above the view mode content. Only visible when at least one recurring group exists. Collapsible via a "Hide/Show" toggle. ### What "View all occurrences" does Clicking "View all occurrences" for a pattern group sets a special URL filter param (e.g., `?pattern=`) that filters the insight list to show only insights in that group. The filter appears as a removable chip. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/components/insights/RecurringPatternsSection.tsx` | **NEW** | ~120 | | `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Modify -- add RecurringPatternsSection above view content, add pattern filter logic | ~30 lines changed | ### Complexity Medium. The Union-Find grouping logic is the trickiest part. A simpler approach: instead of full Union-Find, just use the first insight's `linked_insight_ids` as the canonical group (each insight lists its linked siblings). Since the LLM analysis writes the same set of IDs to all members of a group, we can deduplicate by sorting the linked ID sets. ### Dependencies Depends on Item 1 (URL-persisted filters) for the `pattern` filter param. Should come after Item 4 (view modes) since it integrates into the InsightsPage layout. --- ## Task Breakdown and Dependency Graph ``` Item 1: URL-Persisted Filters (FOUNDATION) | +---> Item 3: Session Detail Sidebar (depends on Item 1 for "Same project" link) | +---> Item 4: Insights View Modes (depends on Item 1 for view mode persistence) | | | +---> Item 6: Recurring Patterns (depends on Item 4 for InsightsPage layout) | Item 2: Progress Ring (INDEPENDENT -- no dependencies) Item 5: Conversation Search (INDEPENDENT -- no dependencies) ``` ### Implementation Order 1. **Item 1: URL-Persisted Filters** -- Foundation for Items 3, 4, 6 2. **Item 2: Progress Ring** -- Can be done in parallel with Item 1 3. **Item 3: Session Detail Sidebar** -- After Item 1 4. **Item 4: Insights View Modes** -- After Item 1 5. **Item 5: Conversation Search** -- Any time (independent) 6. **Item 6: Recurring Patterns** -- After Item 4 ### Parallelization ``` Phase A (parallel): Item 1 (useFilterParams) + Item 2 (ProgressRing) Phase B (parallel): Item 3 (Sidebar) + Item 4 (View Modes) + Item 5 (Conversation Search) Phase C (after B): Item 6 (Recurring Patterns) ``` ### Agent Assignment | Task | Recommended Agent | Rationale | |------|-------------------|-----------| | Item 1: useFilterParams hook | `engineer` | Pure hook logic, no UX decisions | | Item 1: Integrate into SessionsPage | `engineer` | Mechanical replacement | | Item 1: Integrate into InsightsPage | `engineer` | Mechanical replacement | | Item 2: ProgressRing component | `ux-engineer` | SVG visual component | | Item 2: Integrate into PromptQualityCard | `ux-engineer` | Visual refinement | | Item 3: SessionSidebar component | `ux-engineer` | Layout design, responsive behavior | | Item 3: Restructure SessionDetailPage | `engineer` | Significant page restructure | | Item 4: View modes + grouping logic | `engineer` | Data grouping logic | | Item 4: View mode UI/layout | `ux-engineer` | Visual grouped sections | | Item 5: ConversationSearch component | `engineer` | Search logic, scroll management | | Item 6: RecurringPatternsSection | `engineer` | Client-side grouping logic | Given the typical pattern of using a single `engineer` agent for implementation, all items can be assigned to `engineer` with the ProgressRing and sidebar layout being straightforward enough to not require `ux-engineer` specifically. ### Total Estimated Effort | Item | New Files | Modified Files | New Lines | Changed Lines | |------|-----------|---------------|-----------|---------------| | 1. URL Filters | 1 | 2 | ~50 | ~55 | | 2. Progress Ring | 1 | 1 | ~45 | ~10 | | 3. Sidebar Layout | 1 | 1 | ~150 | ~80 | | 4. View Modes | 0 | 2 | ~150 | ~25 | | 5. Conversation Search | 1 | 2 | ~80 | ~40 | | 6. Recurring Patterns | 1 | 1 | ~120 | ~30 | | **TOTAL** | **5** | **9** (some overlap) | **~595** | **~240** | Unique files touched: ~10 (5 new + 5 existing -- SessionsPage, InsightsPage, SessionDetailPage, PromptQualityCard, ChatConversation, plus utils.ts). --- ## Additional Notes ### No API/Schema Changes Required All 6 items work with the existing API endpoints and data model: - URL filter persistence is purely client-side - Progress ring uses existing `efficiencyScore` metadata - Sidebar extracts existing session data into a new layout - View modes group existing insight data client-side - Conversation search operates on already-fetched messages client-side - Recurring patterns use existing `linked_insight_ids` field ### Bundle Size Impact - ProgressRing: ~45 lines of code, zero new dependencies - useFilterParams: ~50 lines, uses existing react-router - All other items are component code using existing deps (React, Tailwind, shadcn/ui) - No new npm packages required - Estimated bundle impact: <5KB uncompressed, negligible on the current 2,030KB / 641KB gzip baseline ### Dark Mode All items use the existing Tailwind class-based dark mode approach: - toolUseResult.prompt:
## Context

We're planning Tier 2 of the Sessions & Insights page redesign for Code Insights dashboard. Tier 1 was completed (type-specific insight cards, enhanced session rows, session detail improvements). Now we need the structural/functional improvements.

## Current State (from exploration)

### Pages:
- **SessionDetailPage.tsx** (640 lines): Full-height flex column, tabs (Overview/Conversation), no sidebar, no conversation search. Uses `useSession(id)`, `useInsights({sessionId})`, `useMessages(id)` with infinite scroll.
- **InsightsPage.tsx** (207 lines): Single column, list/grid toggle, filters (search, project, type) — all local useState, NOT URL-persisted. Uses `useInsights()`, `useProjects()`.
- **SessionsPage.tsx** (423 lines): Single column, date-grouped session rows, filters (search, project, character, status, source) — all local useState, NOT URL-persisted.

### Key Technical Facts:
- **No URL filter persistence anywhere** — all pages use useState for filters
- **No sidebar components exist** in the dashboard
- **No conversation search** — ChatConversation.tsx (125 lines) has infinite scroll but no search
- **Recharts v3.7.0** available but not used in detail pages
- **React Router v7.2.0** available (has useSearchParams)
- **shadcn/ui** has ResizablePanel available (radix-based)
- **linked_insight_ids** field exists on insights for recurring patterns but is only shown as "Recurring Nx" badge
- **PromptQualityCard.tsx** (239 lines) shows score as large text number, no progress ring

### Available Dependencies:
- react-router v7.2.0 (useSearchParams for URL state)
- recharts v3.7.0 (RadialBarChart for progress ring)
- shadcn/ui (ResizablePanel, Tabs, etc.)
- date-fns v4.1.0
- lucide-react icons

## Tier 2 Scope Items

1. **Session detail sidebar layout** — Fixed vitals panel alongside main content
2. **Insights page view modes** — Timeline, By Type, By Project, By Session
3. **URL-persisted filters** — Browser back/forward preserves filter state on Sessions and Insights pages
4. **Conversation search** — Search within session messages
5. **Recurring patterns section** — Group and display linked insights on Insights page
6. **Prompt quality progress ring** — SVG circular progress visualization for score

## Design Constraints

- Must preserve existing functionality
- Should use existing shadcn/ui components where possible
- Dashboard types use snake_case (matching SQLite/API)
- Dark mode must work (class-based)
- Keep bundle size reasonable (currently 2,030KB / 641KB gzip)
- Don't over-engineer — minimum complexity for current needs
- No API/schema changes unless absolutely necessary

## Planning Requirements

Create a detailed implementation plan covering:

### For each Tier 2 item:
1. **Files to modify/create** (with paths)
2. **Implementation approach** — specific components, hooks, patterns
3. **Dependencies between items** — what must come first?
4. **Estimated complexity** (lines of code, number of files)

### Specific design decisions needed:

**Sidebar layout:**
- Should we use shadcn ResizablePanel or a simpler fixed-width CSS sidebar?
- What goes in the sidebar vs main content? (Vitals, insight summary, navigation)
- How does it collapse on mobile?

**URL-persisted filters:**
- Custom hook pattern (useFilterParams) or inline useSearchParams?
- Which filters persist? All of them? Just the important ones?
- How to handle default values (don't pollute URL with defaults)?

**Insights view modes:**
- How to implement the tab-based mode switcher (Timeline/By Type/By Project/By Session)?
- What grouping logic for each mode?
- Should view mode also persist in URL?

**Conversation search:**
- Client-side search across already-loaded messages or needs new API endpoint?
- How to handle infinite scroll + search (need all messages loaded)?
- Highlight approach for matching text?

**Recurring patterns:**
- How to group linked insights? Client-side from linked_insight_ids?
- Where to show them (top of Insights page? Separate tab?)?
- Need API changes to efficiently query recurring patterns?

**Progress ring:**
- SVG custom component or Recharts RadialBarChart?
- Size and placement within PromptQualityCard?

### Task breakdown:
- Break into independently implementable tasks
- Identify which can be parallelized
- Suggest assignment strategy (which agent: UX-engineer vs engineer)

Output a comprehensive implementation plan ready for execution.

The ring replaces the number+"/100" display while keeping the label/description to its right. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/components/shared/ProgressRing.tsx` | **NEW** | ~45 | | `dashboard/src/components/insights/PromptQualityCard.tsx` | Modify -- swap score display for ProgressRing | ~10 lines changed | ### Complexity Low. Self-contained component, minimal integration surface. --- ## 3. Session Detail Sidebar Layout ### Design Decision: shadcn ResizablePanel vs fixed-width CSS sidebar **Recommendation: Fixed-width CSS sidebar (no ResizablePanel).** Rationale: - ResizablePanel (from `react-resizable-panels`) is not installed. It would be a new dependency (`radix-ui` v1.4.3 is installed but ResizablePanel comes from a separate `react-resizable-panels` package). - The sidebar content has a known, fixed structure (vitals, insight summary, navigation). There is no user need to resize it. - A simple flexbox layout (`flex flex-row` with a `w-72 shrink-0` sidebar and `flex-1` main) is sufficient and zero-dependency. - The UX doc specifies 280px (w-70) which is close to `w-72` (288px) in Tailwind. ### Layout structure ```
{/* Sidebar -- hidden on mobile, shown on lg+ */}
{/* Main content */}
{/* Header stays here */} {/* Tabs stay here */}
``` ### Mobile collapse On screens below `lg` (1024px), the sidebar is hidden via `hidden lg:flex`. The vitals data is already shown in the header metadata row and the Overview tab's Session Vitals grid, so mobile users don't lose access to the data. No separate mobile pill strip is needed for Tier 2 -- that is a Tier 3 polish item. ### SessionSidebar component content Extract from the current Overview tab into the sidebar: - **Session Vitals**: Duration, Messages (with user/assistant breakdown), Tool calls, Character - **Cost & Model**: Estimated cost, Primary model, All models used - **Branch & Source**: Git branch, Source tool - **Outcome**: Outcome badge (if analyzed) - **Insight Summary**: Count by type (e.g., "2 Learnings, 1 Decision"), clickable to scroll main content - **Quick Navigate**: "Same project" link (links to `/sessions?project=` -- uses URL-persisted filters from item 1) ### What stays in the main content Overview tab - Summary section (with bullets) - PR links section - Insights (learnings, decisions, prompt quality) -- the actual cards The vitals grid (currently 4 cards) and Usage Stats section are **removed from Overview** and moved to sidebar. This reduces the Overview scroll depth significantly. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/components/sessions/SessionSidebar.tsx` | **NEW** | ~150 | | `dashboard/src/pages/SessionDetailPage.tsx` | Modify -- wrap in flex layout, move vitals to sidebar, remove vitals from overview | ~80 lines changed (significant restructure) | ### Complexity Medium. The SessionDetailPage is 640 lines and the restructure touches the outer layout (changing from single-column to two-column) and moves significant JSX blocks. But it is a pure layout change with no logic changes. ### Dependencies Depends on Item 1 (URL-persisted filters) for the "Same project" navigation link in the sidebar. --- ## 4. Insights Page View Modes ### Design Decision: How to implement the mode switcher **Recommendation: Tabs-like segmented control using shadcn Tabs component (already installed).** The existing `Tabs` component from shadcn/ui is already used in SessionDetailPage. Use it for the view mode selector: ```tsx Timeline By Type By Project By Session ``` The `viewMode` comes from `useFilterParams` (URL-persisted, default: "timeline"). ### Grouping logic per mode All grouping is **client-side** since `useInsights()` already fetches all insights. No API changes needed. **Timeline (default):** Group by date (Today, Yesterday, This Week, Earlier -- matching the pattern in SessionsPage's `getDateGroup`). Uses the existing chronological order. **By Type:** Group insights by `type` field. Within each group, sort by `timestamp` DESC. Show sections with headers: "Decisions (8)", "Learnings (12)", etc. Each section is a collapsible container. Type-specific cards render within their section. **By Project:** Group insights by `project_name`. Within each group, sort by `timestamp` DESC. Show project name as section header with insight count. **By Session:** Group insights by `session_id`. Need to show session title for each group. The insight objects already have `session_id` but not session title. Two options: - Option A: Use `session_id` as the group key and show the first insight's `project_name` + timestamp as the section header (approximate, since we don't have session title in the insight response) - Option B: Cross-reference with sessions data by also calling `useSessions()` to get titles **Recommendation: Option A** for simplicity. We can show "Session from [project_name] -- [formatted timestamp]" as the group header. Fetching all sessions just for titles adds an unnecessary query. ### View mode URL persistence The `view` param is included in `useFilterParams` for InsightsPage. Default is `timeline` (not written to URL). Switching to "type" sets URL to `/insights?view=type`. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Modify -- add view mode tabs, grouping logic, grouped rendering | ~150 lines added | | `dashboard/src/lib/utils.ts` | Modify -- extract `getDateGroup` from SessionsPage to shared utility (or duplicate) | ~15 lines | ### Complexity Medium. The grouping logic is straightforward `Array.reduce` / `Map` operations. The rendering is repetitive (each mode renders the same InsightCard/InsightListItem components, just grouped differently). The bulk of the work is in the InsightsPage template changes. ### Dependencies Depends on Item 1 (URL-persisted filters) for persisting the view mode. --- ## 5. Conversation Search ### Design Decision: Client-side vs server-side search **Recommendation: Client-side search with "load all messages" approach.** Rationale: - The current `useMessages` hook uses infinite scroll (50 messages per page). For search, we need all messages loaded. - Adding a server-side search endpoint would require: new Hono route, SQLite FTS setup (or LIKE query), and a different response format. This is significant effort for a Tier 2 item. - Client-side approach: when the user activates search, load all remaining messages (call `fetchNextPage` until done), then filter client-side. - Most sessions have <200 messages. Even at 200 messages, the data payload is manageable. ### Approach Add a search bar at the top of the Conversation tab in SessionDetailPage: ```tsx // In the Conversation TabsContent
setSearchMatches(matchIndices)} />
``` **ConversationSearch component:** - Text input with search icon - When user types, searches across `message.content` using case-insensitive `includes` - Shows match count ("3 of 12 matches") - Up/Down arrow buttons to navigate between matches - Current match index tracked in state - When navigating to a match, scroll to the corresponding `#msg-{id}` element (already exists in ChatConversation.tsx line 88) **Load-all behavior:** - When search is activated (first character typed), if `hasMore` is true, trigger a loop to load all pages. Show a subtle loading indicator while loading. - Once all messages are loaded, search proceeds client-side. - A ref tracks whether full load has been triggered to avoid re-loading. **Highlight approach:** - The ConversationSearch component passes a `highlightTerm` prop down to ChatConversation - ChatConversation passes it to MessageBubble - MessageBubble's markdown rendering applies a `` wrapper around matching text segments - This is non-trivial for the markdown renderer. **Simpler alternative:** just scroll to matching messages and add a visual indicator (e.g., a left border highlight) without inline text highlighting. This is sufficient for Tier 2. **Recommendation for Tier 2:** Scroll-to-match with message-level highlight (border/background), NOT inline text highlighting. Inline highlighting in markdown-rendered content is a Tier 3 item. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/components/chat/conversation/ConversationSearch.tsx` | **NEW** | ~80 | | `dashboard/src/components/chat/conversation/ChatConversation.tsx` | Modify -- accept `highlightMessageId` prop, apply highlight class | ~15 lines changed | | `dashboard/src/pages/SessionDetailPage.tsx` | Modify -- add search state, pass to ChatConversation | ~25 lines changed | ### Complexity Medium. The search logic itself is simple. The tricky parts are: 1. Loading all messages when search activates (managing the "load all" loop) 2. Scrolling to specific messages (using `document.getElementById('msg-...')?.scrollIntoView()`) 3. Keeping the search bar sticky while the conversation scrolls ### Dependencies None -- this is self-contained within SessionDetailPage. --- ## 6. Recurring Patterns Section ### Design Decision: How to group linked insights **Recommendation: Client-side grouping from `linked_insight_ids` field.** The `linked_insight_ids` field is a JSON-encoded string array on each insight. Insights that share linked IDs are part of the same recurring pattern. The grouping logic: 1. Parse `linked_insight_ids` for each insight 2. Build a Union-Find / adjacency structure: if insight A lists insight B, they are in the same group 3. From each group, extract: the theme (from the insight title), the count, the project names, the most recent timestamp **Alternative:** The `findRecurringInsights` API already exists (POST `/api/analysis/recurring`). It uses LLM to identify recurring patterns. However: - This requires LLM to be configured - It is an active analysis operation, not a read operation - It returns groups and updates `linked_insight_ids` in the database **Recommendation:** Use the already-stored `linked_insight_ids` for the display (client-side grouping). Show a "Find Patterns" button that triggers the `findRecurringInsights` API to populate/refresh the linked IDs. This way the section works with existing data and can be refreshed on demand. ### Where to show Show at the top of InsightsPage, above the view mode content. Only visible when at least one recurring group exists. Collapsible via a "Hide/Show" toggle. ### What "View all occurrences" does Clicking "View all occurrences" for a pattern group sets a special URL filter param (e.g., `?pattern=`) that filters the insight list to show only insights in that group. The filter appears as a removable chip. ### Files to modify/create | File | Action | Estimated Lines | |------|--------|----------------| | `dashboard/src/components/insights/RecurringPatternsSection.tsx` | **NEW** | ~120 | | `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Modify -- add RecurringPatternsSection above view content, add pattern filter logic | ~30 lines changed | ### Complexity Medium. The Union-Find grouping logic is the trickiest part. A simpler approach: instead of full Union-Find, just use the first insight's `linked_insight_ids` as the canonical group (each insight lists its linked siblings). Since the LLM analysis writes the same set of IDs to all members of a group, we can deduplicate by sorting the linked ID sets. ### Dependencies Depends on Item 1 (URL-persisted filters) for the `pattern` filter param. Should come after Item 4 (view modes) since it integrates into the InsightsPage layout. --- ## Task Breakdown and Dependency Graph ``` Item 1: URL-Persisted Filters (FOUNDATION) | +---> Item 3: Session Detail Sidebar (depends on Item 1 for "Same project" link) | +---> Item 4: Insights View Modes (depends on Item 1 for view mode persistence) | | | +---> Item 6: Recurring Patterns (depends on Item 4 for InsightsPage layout) | Item 2: Progress Ring (INDEPENDENT -- no dependencies) Item 5: Conversation Search (INDEPENDENT -- no dependencies) ``` ### Implementation Order 1. **Item 1: URL-Persisted Filters** -- Foundation for Items 3, 4, 6 2. **Item 2: Progress Ring** -- Can be done in parallel with Item 1 3. **Item 3: Session Detail Sidebar** -- After Item 1 4. **Item 4: Insights View Modes** -- After Item 1 5. **Item 5: Conversation Search** -- Any time (independent) 6. **Item 6: Recurring Patterns** -- After Item 4 ### Parallelization ``` Phase A (parallel): Item 1 (useFilterParams) + Item 2 (ProgressRing) Phase B (parallel): Item 3 (Sidebar) + Item 4 (View Modes) + Item 5 (Conversation Search) Phase C (after B): Item 6 (Recurring Patterns) ``` ### Agent Assignment | Task | Recommended Agent | Rationale | |------|-------------------|-----------| | Item 1: useFilterParams hook | `engineer` | Pure hook logic, no UX decisions | | Item 1: Integrate into SessionsPage | `engineer` | Mechanical replacement | | Item 1: Integrate into InsightsPage | `engineer` | Mechanical replacement | | Item 2: ProgressRing component | `ux-engineer` | SVG visual component | | Item 2: Integrate into PromptQualityCard | `ux-engineer` | Visual refinement | | Item 3: SessionSidebar component | `ux-engineer` | Layout design, responsive behavior | | Item 3: Restructure SessionDetailPage | `engineer` | Significant page restructure | | Item 4: View modes + grouping logic | `engineer` | Data grouping logic | | Item 4: View mode UI/layout | `ux-engineer` | Visual grouped sections | | Item 5: ConversationSearch component | `engineer` | Search logic, scroll management | | Item 6: RecurringPatternsSection | `engineer` | Client-side grouping logic | Given the typical pattern of using a single `engineer` agent for implementation, all items can be assigned to `engineer` with the ProgressRing and sidebar layout being straightforward enough to not require `ux-engineer` specifically. ### Total Estimated Effort | Item | New Files | Modified Files | New Lines | Changed Lines | |------|-----------|---------------|-----------|---------------| | 1. URL Filters | 1 | 2 | ~50 | ~55 | | 2. Progress Ring | 1 | 1 | ~45 | ~10 | | 3. Sidebar Layout | 1 | 1 | ~150 | ~80 | | 4. View Modes | 0 | 2 | ~150 | ~25 | | 5. Conversation Search | 1 | 2 | ~80 | ~40 | | 6. Recurring Patterns | 1 | 1 | ~120 | ~30 | | **TOTAL** | **5** | **9** (some overlap) | **~595** | **~240** | Unique files touched: ~10 (5 new + 5 existing -- SessionsPage, InsightsPage, SessionDetailPage, PromptQualityCard, ChatConversation, plus utils.ts). --- ## Additional Notes ### No API/Schema Changes Required All 6 items work with the existing API endpoints and data model: - URL filter persistence is purely client-side - Progress ring uses existing `efficiencyScore` metadata - Sidebar extracts existing session data into a new layout - View modes group existing insight data client-side - Conversation search operates on already-fetched messages client-side - Recurring patterns use existing `linked_insight_ids` field ### Bundle Size Impact - ProgressRing: ~45 lines of code, zero new dependencies - useFilterParams: ~50 lines, uses existing react-router - All other items are component code using existing deps (React, Tailwind, shadcn/ui) - No new npm packages required - Estimated bundle impact: <5KB uncompressed, negligible on the current 2,030KB / 641KB gzip baseline ### Dark Mode All items use the existing Tailwind class-based dark mode approach: - SVG colors in ProgressRing use the same color mapping as existing `getScoreColor` (hex values that work in both modes) - Sidebar uses `border-r`, `bg-background`, `text-muted-foreground` -- all theme-aware - No new color system needed --- ### Critical Files for Implementation - `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/dashboard/src/pages/SessionDetailPage.tsx` - Heaviest modification: sidebar restructure + conversation search integration (640 lines currently) - `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` - View modes, recurring patterns section, URL filters (207 lines currently, will roughly double) - `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/dashboard/src/pages/SessionsPage.tsx` - URL filter integration replacing useState (423 lines) - `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/dashboard/src/components/insights/PromptQualityCard.tsx` - Progress ring integration (239 lines) - `/Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/dashboard/src/hooks/useFilterParams.ts` - New foundation hook used by 2 pages (to be created)

input.content:
# Tier 2: Sessions & Insights Page Redesign

## Context

Tier 1 (PR #95, merged) surfaced rich metadata in type-specific insight cards and enhanced session rows. Tier 2 adds structural/functional improvements: sidebar layout, view modes, URL-persisted filters, conversation search, recurring patterns, and a progress ring.

**All items are frontend-only** — no API or schema changes needed. Estimated: 5 new files, ~5 modified files, ~595 new lines.

---

## Dependency Graph

```
Phase A (parallel):  1. URL Filters + 2. Progress Ring
Phase B (parallel):  3. Sidebar + 4. View Modes + 5. Conversation Search
Phase C (after 4):   6. Recurring Patterns
```

---

## Item 1: URL-Persisted Filters (FOUNDATION)

**Why first:** Items 3, 4, 6 depend on URL state for navigation links and view mode persistence.

**Approach:** Create `useFilterParams<T>` hook wrapping React Router's `useSearchParams`. Only non-default values written to URL (clean URLs). Search input uses `replace: true` to avoid history pollution; dropdowns use `push`.

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/hooks/useFilterParams.ts` | **NEW** (~50 lines) — generic hook |
| `dashboard/src/pages/SessionsPage.tsx` | Replace 5 `useState` calls with `useFilterParams` |
| `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Replace 4 `useState` calls with `useFilterParams` |

**Persisted filters:**
- SessionsPage: `q`, `project`, `source`, `character`, `status`
- InsightsPage: `q`, `project`, `type`, `view` (new view mode param)

---

## Item 2: Prompt Quality Progress Ring

**Approach:** Custom SVG component (~45 lines, zero new deps). Uses `strokeDasharray`/`strokeDashoffset` for the arc. Color mapped from score matching existing `getScoreColor`. Replaces the large text number in PromptQualityCard.

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/components/shared/ProgressRing.tsx` | **NEW** (~45 lines) |
| `dashboard/src/components/insights/PromptQualityCard.tsx` | Swap text score for `<ProgressRing>` |

---

## Item 3: Session Detail Sidebar

**Approach:** Fixed-width CSS sidebar (`w-72 shrink-0`, flexbox) — no new dependencies (no ResizablePanel). Hidden below `lg` breakpoint via `hidden lg:flex`. Move vitals + usage stats from Overview tab into sidebar so they're always visible.

**Sidebar content:** Duration, messages (user/assistant), tool calls, cost, model(s), branch, source, outcome badge, insight summary counts, "Same project" link (uses URL filters from Item 1).

**Overview tab after move:** Summary + PR links + Insight cards only (shorter scroll).

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/components/sessions/SessionSidebar.tsx` | **NEW** (~150 lines) |
| `dashboard/src/pages/SessionDetailPage.tsx` | Wrap in flex layout, move vitals/usage to sidebar |

---

## Item 4: Insights Page View Modes

**Approach:** Segmented control using existing shadcn `Tabs` component. Four modes with client-side grouping:

| Mode | Grouping | Section Headers |
|------|----------|-----------------|
| Timeline (default) | By date (Today/Yesterday/This Week/Earlier) | Date labels with count |
| By Type | By `insight.type` | "Decisions (8)", "Learnings (12)", etc. |
| By Project | By `project_name` | Project name + insight count |
| By Session | By `session_id` | Project name + timestamp |

Extract `getDateGroup` from SessionsPage to shared utility (reuse in both pages).

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Add Tabs-based mode switcher + grouping logic (~150 lines added) |
| `dashboard/src/lib/utils.ts` | Extract `getDateGroup` helper from SessionsPage |
| `dashboard/src/pages/SessionsPage.tsx` | Import shared `getDateGroup` instead of inline |

---

## Item 5: Conversation Search

**Approach:** Client-side search. When user types in search bar, load all remaining messages (loop `fetchNextPage` until done), then filter by `message.content.includes(term)`.

**UX:** Search bar sticky at top of Conversation tab. Shows "N matches" with prev/next navigation buttons. Matching messages get a highlighted border/background (NOT inline text highlighting — that's Tier 3). Scrolls to current match via `scrollIntoView`.

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/components/chat/conversation/ConversationSearch.tsx` | **NEW** (~80 lines) |
| `dashboard/src/components/chat/conversation/ChatConversation.tsx` | Accept `highlightMessageId` prop |
| `dashboard/src/pages/SessionDetailPage.tsx` | Add search state, wire to ConversationSearch |

---

## Item 6: Recurring Patterns Section

**Approach:** Client-side grouping from existing `linked_insight_ids` field. Group insights that share linked IDs. Show a "Recurring Patterns" section at top of InsightsPage (above view mode content) when groups exist.

Each pattern card shows: theme (title), occurrence count, project names, last seen date, "View all" button that filters the list via URL param (`?pattern=<groupKey>`).

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/components/insights/RecurringPatternsSection.tsx` | **NEW** (~120 lines) |
| `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Add section above view content + pattern filter logic |

---

## Execution Strategy

**Team:** `/start-feature` with PM coordinating. Single `ux-engineer` agent implements all items (primarily dashboard UI work with some hook logic).

**Phases:**
- **Phase A:** Items 1 + 2 in parallel (independent)
- **Phase B:** Items 3 + 4 + 5 (all unblocked after Item 1, touch different files)
- **Phase C:** Item 6 (depends on Item 4 for InsightsPage layout)
- **Phase D:** CI gate + PR creation
- **Phase E:** Triple-layer code review

**Branch:** `feature/sessions-insights-tier2`

---

## Verification

1. `pnpm build` passes across all packages
2. Navigate to `/sessions` — filter by project, refresh page — filters persist in URL
3. Navigate to `/sessions/:id` — sidebar shows vitals, main content has tabs
4. On session detail Conversation tab — search for a keyword, matches highlighted
5. Navigate to `/insights` — switch between Timeline/By Type/By Project/By Session views
6. On Insights page — recurring patterns section shows at top (if data exists)
7. Prompt quality cards show circular progress ring instead of text number
8. All pages work in dark mode
9. Mobile/tablet: sidebar hidden, pages still functional

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/melagiri/.claude/plans/hazy-whistling-alpaca.md

input.plan:
# Tier 2: Sessions & Insights Page Redesign

## Context

Tier 1 (PR #95, merged) surfaced rich metadata in type-specific insight cards and enhanced session rows. Tier 2 adds structural/functional improvements: sidebar layout, view modes, URL-persisted filters, conversation search, recurring patterns, and a progress ring.

**All items are frontend-only** — no API or schema changes needed. Estimated: 5 new files, ~5 modified files, ~595 new lines.

---

## Dependency Graph

```
Phase A (parallel):  1. URL Filters + 2. Progress Ring
Phase B (parallel):  3. Sidebar + 4. View Modes + 5. Conversation Search
Phase C (after 4):   6. Recurring Patterns
```

---

## Item 1: URL-Persisted Filters (FOUNDATION)

**Why first:** Items 3, 4, 6 depend on URL state for navigation links and view mode persistence.

**Approach:** Create `useFilterParams<T>` hook wrapping React Router's `useSearchParams`. Only non-default values written to URL (clean URLs). Search input uses `replace: true` to avoid history pollution; dropdowns use `push`.

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/hooks/useFilterParams.ts` | **NEW** (~50 lines) — generic hook |
| `dashboard/src/pages/SessionsPage.tsx` | Replace 5 `useState` calls with `useFilterParams` |
| `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Replace 4 `useState` calls with `useFilterParams` |

**Persisted filters:**
- SessionsPage: `q`, `project`, `source`, `character`, `status`
- InsightsPage: `q`, `project`, `type`, `view` (new view mode param)

---

## Item 2: Prompt Quality Progress Ring

**Approach:** Custom SVG component (~45 lines, zero new deps). Uses `strokeDasharray`/`strokeDashoffset` for the arc. Color mapped from score matching existing `getScoreColor`. Replaces the large text number in PromptQualityCard.

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/components/shared/ProgressRing.tsx` | **NEW** (~45 lines) |
| `dashboard/src/components/insights/PromptQualityCard.tsx` | Swap text score for `<ProgressRing>` |

---

## Item 3: Session Detail Sidebar

**Approach:** Fixed-width CSS sidebar (`w-72 shrink-0`, flexbox) — no new dependencies (no ResizablePanel). Hidden below `lg` breakpoint via `hidden lg:flex`. Move vitals + usage stats from Overview tab into sidebar so they're always visible.

**Sidebar content:** Duration, messages (user/assistant), tool calls, cost, model(s), branch, source, outcome badge, insight summary counts, "Same project" link (uses URL filters from Item 1).

**Overview tab after move:** Summary + PR links + Insight cards only (shorter scroll).

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/components/sessions/SessionSidebar.tsx` | **NEW** (~150 lines) |
| `dashboard/src/pages/SessionDetailPage.tsx` | Wrap in flex layout, move vitals/usage to sidebar |

---

## Item 4: Insights Page View Modes

**Approach:** Segmented control using existing shadcn `Tabs` component. Four modes with client-side grouping:

| Mode | Grouping | Section Headers |
|------|----------|-----------------|
| Timeline (default) | By date (Today/Yesterday/This Week/Earlier) | Date labels with count |
| By Type | By `insight.type` | "Decisions (8)", "Learnings (12)", etc. |
| By Project | By `project_name` | Project name + insight count |
| By Session | By `session_id` | Project name + timestamp |

Extract `getDateGroup` from SessionsPage to shared utility (reuse in both pages).

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Add Tabs-based mode switcher + grouping logic (~150 lines added) |
| `dashboard/src/lib/utils.ts` | Extract `getDateGroup` helper from SessionsPage |
| `dashboard/src/pages/SessionsPage.tsx` | Import shared `getDateGroup` instead of inline |

---

## Item 5: Conversation Search

**Approach:** Client-side search. When user types in search bar, load all remaining messages (loop `fetchNextPage` until done), then filter by `message.content.includes(term)`.

**UX:** Search bar sticky at top of Conversation tab. Shows "N matches" with prev/next navigation buttons. Matching messages get a highlighted border/background (NOT inline text highlighting — that's Tier 3). Scrolls to current match via `scrollIntoView`.

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/components/chat/conversation/ConversationSearch.tsx` | **NEW** (~80 lines) |
| `dashboard/src/components/chat/conversation/ChatConversation.tsx` | Accept `highlightMessageId` prop |
| `dashboard/src/pages/SessionDetailPage.tsx` | Add search state, wire to ConversationSearch |

---

## Item 6: Recurring Patterns Section

**Approach:** Client-side grouping from existing `linked_insight_ids` field. Group insights that share linked IDs. Show a "Recurring Patterns" section at top of InsightsPage (above view mode content) when groups exist.

Each pattern card shows: theme (title), occurrence count, project names, last seen date, "View all" button that filters the list via URL param (`?pattern=<groupKey>`).

**Files:**
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `dashboard/src/components/insights/RecurringPatternsSection.tsx` | **NEW** (~120 lines) |
| `dashboard/src/pages/InsightsPage.tsx` | Add section above view content + pattern filter logic |

---

## Execution Strategy

**Team:** `/start-feature` with PM coordinating. Single `ux-engineer` agent implements all items (primarily dashboard UI work with some hook logic).

**Phases:**
- **Phase A:** Items 1 + 2 in parallel (independent)
- **Phase B:** Items 3 + 4 + 5 (all unblocked after Item 1, touch different files)
- **Phase C:** Item 6 (depends on Item 4 for InsightsPage layout)
- **Phase D:** CI gate + PR creation
- **Phase E:** Triple-layer code review

**Branch:** `feature/sessions-insights-tier2`

---

## Verification

1. `pnpm build` passes across all packages
2. Navigate to `/sessions` — filter by project, refresh page — filters persist in URL
3. Navigate to `/sessions/:id` — sidebar shows vitals, main content has tabs
4. On session detail Conversation tab — search for a keyword, matches highlighted
5. Navigate to `/insights` — switch between Timeline/By Type/By Project/By Session views
6. On Insights page — recurring patterns section shows at top (if data exists)
7. Prompt quality cards show circular progress ring instead of text number
8. All pages work in dark mode
9. Mobile/tablet: sidebar hidden, pages still functional
